rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA ALICE JACKSON et al VS. ROSE L. JACKSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Fo Reimbursement Of Expenses And Apportionment And Distribution Of Assets;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 20, 2007, LINE 2. PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA JACKSON AND MARIAN JACKSON-CHAVEZ'S Motion FoR Reimbursement Of Expenses And Apportionment And Distribution Of Assets IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 22, 2008. MOVING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS ALONG WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: TAYLOR KOROBOW VS. SUSAN HARDIN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 20, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT DOUGLAS ROSS CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BY DEMONSTRATING THAT THE DOOR WAS OPENED ON SEVERAL OCCASIONS PRIOR TO THE ACCIDENT. PLAINTIFF DID NOT PRESENT TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF'S OWN DECLARATION CONFIRMS THAT SHE DISCOVERED THE DOOR STUCK PRIOR TO HER INJURY AND THE DECLARATION OF LUNEY CONFIRMS DEFENDANT'S FACTUAL SHOWING THAT A REASONABLE INSPECTION WOULD INVOLVE OPENING THE DOOR AND SUCH AN ACT HAD OCCURRED ON SEVERAL OCCASIONS PRIOR TO THE ACCIDENT. THE OBJECTION TO PARAGRAPH SEVEN OF THE PERRY DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED. THE OBJECTION TO PARAGRAPH EIGHT OF THE LUNEY DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: W. D. FLIENT VS. EDNA MALUNAY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 20, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANT EDNA MALUNAY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 23, 2008 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: NATALIE PERTSOVSKY VS. PETER CHASE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 20, 2007, LINE 5. DEFENDANT ARUN SHARMA B.D.S., M.S.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON THE JACOBSON DECLARATION. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT COMPETENT EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. MUNRO V U.C. REGENTS (1989) 215 CAL APP 3D 977, 985. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' et al VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 20, 2007, LINE 13. DEFENDANTS STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINSTRATION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS, BRIGID HANSON, RODERICK HICKMAN AND TIM VIRGA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF AND PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE PROPOSALS AT ISSUE NEVER WERE IMPLEMENTED. AS A CONSEQUENCE, NO CONSTITUTIONAL VIOLATION OCCURRED (ASSUMING ONE COULD ARISE FROM THE FACTS ALLEGED) AND PLAINTIFF SUFFERED NO HARM AS A CONSEQUENCE OF DEFENDANTS' ACTIONS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED A BASIS TO SUE DEFENDANTS IN THEIR OFFICIAL CAPACITY. =(302/PJM/AY)
